titel: Improved opportunities to investigate crimes committed by young offenders, and some other procedural legal matters.
html:
Serious crime is increasingly affecting younger age groups. This trend must be reversed, and children must be protected from exploitation and involvement in criminal activity. A key element in crime prevention efforts is to identify young people who are at risk of adopting a criminal lifestyle and provide them with adequate support. Continuous work on crime prevention measures is essential. Furthermore, from both a societal and a victim's perspective, it is crucial that crimes can be investigated effectively, regardless of the age of the perpetrator. Therefore, there is a need for more effective tools to investigate crimes committed by young offenders.

The government proposes, among other things, the following:

  • More coercive measures should be allowed to be used against children who have not reached the age of criminal responsibility.
  • The coercive measures of search, seizure, and body search should be allowed to be used against children who have not reached the age of criminal responsibility in more cases.
  • Biometric data should be allowed to be collected from children in more cases.

The government also proposes the introduction of a coercive measure for the detention of a group of people and a special regulation for coercive measures in a preliminary investigation that is reopened to investigate whether there is grounds for reopening the case.

The proposed amendments to the law are scheduled to come into force on July 1, 2026.

Proposal for a Parliamentary Decision

Prop. 2025/26:227

Government Proposal:

  1. The Parliament approves the government's proposed amendment to the Code of Judicial Procedure.
  2. The Parliament approves the government's proposed amendment to the Act (1964:167) on special provisions regarding young offenders.
  3. The Parliament approves the government's proposed amendment to the Act (2025:862) amending the Act (1964:167) on special provisions regarding young offenders.
  4. The Parliament approves the government's proposed amendment to the Act (1964:167) on special provisions regarding young offenders.
  5. The Parliament approves the government's proposed amendment to the Act (2007:979) on measures to prevent certain particularly serious crimes.
  6. The Parliament approves the government's proposed amendment to the Act (2007:979) on measures to prevent certain particularly serious crimes.
  7. The Parliament approves the government's proposed amendment to the Act (2018:1693) on the police's handling of personal data within the scope of the criminal data laws.
  8. The Parliament approves the government's proposed amendment to the Act (2020:62) on secret data interception.
  9. The Parliament approves the government's proposed amendment to the Customs Powers Act (2024:710).
  10. The Parliament approves the government's proposed amendment to the Act (2024:782) on the procedure for confiscation of property and imposition of corporate fines.

5
tldr: Serious crime is increasingly involving younger and younger individuals. The government wants to provide the police with more tools to investigate crimes committed by children. This includes allowing the use of more coercive measures, such as searches and seizures, against children below the age of criminal responsibility, in order to protect them from being exploited by criminals.
lattlast:
Serious crime is increasingly involving younger and younger individuals. The government wants to reverse this trend and protect children from being drawn into and exploited by criminal activity.

A key element is to identify young people at risk of a criminal lifestyle early on and provide them with support, but also to be able to effectively investigate crimes regardless of the perpetrator's age.

The government proposes, among other things, that more coercive measures should be allowed to be used against children who have not yet reached the age of criminal responsibility, in order to investigate crimes and protect children.
